headlines.headline: Vance Hails ‘Good Day’ of Iran Talks as US Waives Oil Sanctions
headlines.description: US Vice President JD Vance said talks with Iran over the weekend were “very, very good,” as the sides attempt to reach a peace deal within two months and formally end a war that’s reverberated through the global economy.

Lagarde Sees No Need for More Forceful ECB Response to Iran War
headlines.description: The European Central Bank doesn’t need to react more forcefully to the fallout from the Middle East conflict because inflation is set to return to target over the medium term, President Christine Lagarde said.

headlines.headline: Copper Rallies as Peace-Talk Progress Eases Inflation Fears
headlines.description: Copper prices rose after Iran said there had been “major progress” in peace talks with the US, boosting expectations that traffic through the Strait of Hormuz may recover and ease inflation pressures.

headlines.headline: Former Federal Reserve Chairman Alan Greenspan dies at 100
headlines.description: U.S. Federal Reserve Chair Alan Greenspan has died at the age of 100. He died on Monday from complications of Parkinson’s Disease, said his wife of 29 years, NBC News correspondent Andrea Mitchell. In his 18½ years at the helm of the Fed, Greenspan presided o…

headlines.description: The primary focus of the unrest is a proposed multi-billion-euro tourism project on Albania's Adriatic coast.

headlines.headline: JPMorgan’s Lipikhina Sees Europe Stocks Lagging Despite Oil Drop
headlines.description: European equities may struggle to keep pace with US and emerging market peers in the second half of the year as falling oil prices threaten a key source of earnings growth, according to Nataliia Lipikhina at JPMorgan Chase & Co.

headlines.description: France and Germany agreed on a KNDS framework as Germany seeks a 40% stake ahead of a potential multibillion-euro IPO.

headlines.description: The People’s Bank of China’s latest refinement of its monetary policy toolkit is expected to damp money-market volatility and support bonds, according to analysts.

headlines.description: Artificial intelligence can potentially lead to dangerous financial crises, and the European Central Bank is determined to ensure that doesn’t happen, President Christine Lagarde said.

headlines.description: This is the BOJ's first hike since December, when it raised rates to its current level of 0.75%, also its highest level in over 30 years.
